title: Error when running the language server in Zed

title: Client is not running and can't be stopped.
author: Youdaman
body: Was working fine the other day but since the last update it's no longer showing controllers and is producing errors as per below. Version 1.1.1 running via WSL Ubuntu 24.04 ``` Runtime Status Activation Activated by onView:controllerDefinitions event: 3ms Uncaught Errors (6) Client is not running and can't be stopped. It's current state is: starting Client is not running and can't be stopped. It's current state is: startFailed Pending response rejected since connection got disposed Client is not running and can't be stopped. It's current state is: startFailed Client is not running and can't be stopped. It's current state is: startFailed Client is not running and can't be stopped. It's current state is: startFailed ```

title: Initialization infinite loop on failure causes excessive RAM and CPU usage
author: fiveNinePlusR
body: Potential solutions might be to rate limit the retries, have a maximum number of failures before not retrying again or possibly using an exponential backoff mechanism if that is not desired.

title: Bin file seems to be missing in 1.1.0
author: lukasbischof
body:
Hello there 👋 

The npm package specifies a bin script:

```json
  "bin": {
    "stimulus-language-server": "./out/stimulus-language-server"
  },
```

But in version 1.1.0, it appears that there is no such script `stimulus-language-server` published in the `out` directory?

title: `onDidChangeWatchedFiles` ignores JS/TS controller file changes: LSP index goes stale until restart
author: mickzijdel

title: error for hyphenated values within hyphenated controllers
author: JamesChevalier
body:
I get a `stimulus.controller.value.missing` error for hyphenated values within hyphenated controllers. So if I have a `thing_collection_controller.js` controller with a `show-thing` value: ```js static values = { showThing: { type: Boolean, default: false } } ``` Paired with this HTML: ```html
``` I'll see some red squiggles under the `data-thing-collection-show-thing-value` and this message: > "collectionShowThing" isn't a valid Stimulus Value name on the "thing" controller. Did you mean "0"? > Stimulus LSP (stimulus.controller.value.missing) Let me know if you'd like any more/different info.

title: Action completion should be context-aware
author: janko
body:
When I type in `click->hello#gr`, the language server completion should lock in event and controller, and only offer suggestions for function names in `hello` controller.

Currently, the completion doesn't appear to have notion of what has already been typed out, as top suggestions change the event name after I already entered the `->`. This makes it impossible to safely accept a suggestion, at least in Zed.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/20f2d38b-3bcb-43bf-8a9c-2fb4426b3c72

Like go-to-definition in https://github.com/marcoroth/stimulus-lsp/pull/740, I believe completion should be more context-aware.

title: Diagnostic: Wrap Stimulus target accessor with a presence check
author: marcoroth

title: none of my controllers are "registered" on my Stimulus Application
author: pjmelling
body: I'm getting this warning in all of my Stimulus controllers: `Stimulus: The "dropdown" controller isn't registered on your Stimulus Application` This results in linting errors throughout the codebase that look like `"dropdown" isn't a valid Stimulus controller.` But the app works as expected; my controllers are loaded and run as expected. I whipped up a new vanilla rails app with all the defaults and the plugin doesn't complain at all. So I have to assume it's something with my codebase. I've verified that I have the expected files in the expected locations. I have a `app/javascript/application.js` file, a `app/javascript/controllers/index.js` file, and my controllers all live in `app/javascript/controllers/`. The index.js file looks like this: ``` import { application } from "controllers/application" // Eager load all controllers defined in the import map under controllers/**/*_controller import { eagerLoadControllersFrom } from "@hotwired/stimulus-loading" eagerLoadControllersFrom("controllers", application) eagerLoadControllersFrom("component_controllers", application) ``` **Environment** Ruby: 3.4.1 Rails: 8.0.1 stimulus-rails gem: 1.3.4 Cursor Version: 0.45.15 VSCode Version: 1.96.2 Stimulus LSP plugin: 1.0.4 Where can I look next to resolve this?

title: Support multiple controllers with the same identifier
author: mtownsend81
body:
In our project, we have two controllers with the same identifier, they share a lot of views, targets etc  and essentially do the same job in different contexts, so the identifier makes sense.

The LSP only supports the first found controller here: https://github.com/marcoroth/stimulus-lsp/blob/979f8cbbf72a379131777da5cfe303008187bb89/server/src/diagnostics.ts#L611

So n VS code it's showing errors for values that do exist etc

title: Controllers from external libraries are not recognized
author: smarquez1
body:
Hi, not sure if this is a known issue.

When I use a controller from an external library, such as [this one](https://www.stimulus-components.com/docs/stimulus-rails-nested-form), the LSP does not recognized it.

I'm loading this external library from an importmap btw.

This is the error I get in my `erb` file:

```Stimulus LSP : "nested-form" isn't a valid Stimulus controller. Did you mean "site-selector"? [stimulus.controller.invalid]```

Also, If I create my own controller and inherit from an external one, I'll get a similar error:

```javascript
export default class MyController extends NestedForm {
   ...
}
```

Otherwise this is super useful so thanks for creating it!

title: Path to the Stimulus controllers
author: lgeorgiev
body:
Hello,

Is there any configuration to set the path to the controllers? 
When I use a Rails app it detects it. 
If I use Symfony and Twig, it doesn't detect controllers. It always says that the controller is not valid.
The controllers are located in assets/controllers.

I use Neovim.

title: Add Diagnostic to force implement `static component` for Hotwire Native Bridge Components
author: marcoroth
body:
a) We should prompt users to implement the `static component` on a Hotwire Native/Strada Component.
b) We should make sure the `static component` name matches the Stimulus Controller name it's defined in.
c) If the user defines a Bridge component outside of `app/javascript/controllers/bridge/*` we should prompt them to move it to the `bridge/` folder

Thanks to @yshmarov for brainstorming on this!

title: Add support to detect Hotwire Native Components as Stimulus Controllers
author: marcoroth

title: Add diagnostic for deprecated Strada References
author: marcoroth
body:
https://native.hotwired.dev
https://dev.37signals.com/announcing-hotwire-native/

Hotwire Native was announced, which means that Turbo Native and Strada were deprecated. We should add diagnostics for any imports from the `@hotwired/strada` package and provide quick-fixes to replace the import with `@hotwire/hotwire-native-bridge`.

title: app/javascript/controllers/menu_controller.js not recognized as stimulus controller
author: wdiechmann
body:
in `views/calendars/show.html.erb` my VS Code

```
Version: 1.93.1 (Universal)
Commit: 38c31bc77e0dd6ae88a4e9cc93428cc27a56ba40
Date: 2024-09-11T17:20:05.685Z
Electron: 30.4.0
ElectronBuildId: 10073054
Chromium: 124.0.6367.243
Node.js: 20.15.1
V8: 12.4.254.20-electron.0
OS: Darwin arm64 23.6.0
```

with Stimulus LSP v1.0.4 reports `data-controller="menu"` as 

```
"menu" isn't a valid Stimulus controller. Did you mean "tabs"?
```

even though a `javascript/controllers/menu_controller.js` do exist

is this expected (perhaps because 'menu' is reserved word or something) or is this an anomali ?
